answers.q5: Context-switching between too many communication channels (Slack, email, meetings).
answers.q9: A 2–3 days remote / 2 days in-office hybrid would suit me best. I value focused remote days for deep work and in-person days for brainstorming and 1-on-1s.

answers.q5: Time-zone differences with overseas colleagues.
answers.q9: I thrive fully remote and would like to keep it that way permanently. Occasional in-person offsites (quarterly) energize the team without the daily commute overhead.

answers.q5: Distractions at home (kids, noise, chores) make it hard to focus.
answers.q9: I struggle working from home right now; a 4-day in-office schedule with one remote day would work best for me.

answers.q5: Distractions at home (kids, noise, chores) make it hard to focus.
answers.q9: Honestly, I would prefer going back to the office most days. I need that structure and face-time to stay motivated and learn from colleagues.

answers.q5: Maintaining clear boundaries between work and personal time.
answers.q9: Async-first culture with one optional in-office day per week. No mandatory presence — results over attendance.

answers.q5: Occasional internet outages disrupt video calls.
answers.q9: Async-first culture with one optional in-office day per week. No mandatory presence — results over attendance.

answers.q5: Time-zone differences with overseas colleagues.
answers.q9: Permanently remote with a generous home-office stipend and annual all-hands gathering. That is the sweet spot for focus and culture.

answers.q5: Feeling isolated and disconnected from the team — I miss spontaneous office chat.
answers.q9: I would like a hybrid setup — 3 days in-office and 2 from home. Enough in-person time to feel connected but still some flexibility.

answers.q5: Always-on expectation — messages arrive at all hours and I feel pressure to respond immediately.
answers.q9: Fully remote but with strict no-meeting Wednesdays and an explicit expectation that Slack is not a real-time medium.

answers.q5: Onboarding new team members remotely takes much longer than in-person.
answers.q9: A 2–3 days remote / 2 days in-office hybrid would suit me best. I value focused remote days for deep work and in-person days for brainstorming and 1-on-1s.

answers.q5: Keeping team-wide meetings short enough to stay focused.
answers.q9: Full remote with optional in-person collaboration weeks a few times a year. I value the flexibility to work from anywhere while still meeting colleagues face-to-face.

answers.q5: Keeping documentation up to date so async collaboration actually works.
answers.q9: Remote-first with a well-equipped shared office available whenever I need it. No mandated days, but a great space when I want human contact.

answers.q5: Too many tools, too many notifications — constant context switching drains me.
answers.q9: Remote with strong async norms — no reply expected after 6 PM, no weekend messages, real PTO. That would restore my enthusiasm for remote work.
